history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade evicted or failed to manage com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail and com.alibaba.fastjson2:fastjson2, causing missing com.sun.mail.* implementation classes and JSON.Feature symbol.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a pom_patch step before the SB upgrade to explicitly declare the missing mail and fastjson2 dependencies on the compile classpath, ensuring they survive the parent POM change and dependency management shift.

history.qwen_observation: Dependency resolution failed for com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail:2.1.3 and com.alibaba:fastjson2:2.0.53 due to Maven negative cache and an incorrect groupId for fastjson2.
history.qwen_rationale: Bypass the local Maven negative cache by using slightly older, widely-available versions (2.1.2 and 2.0.43) and correct the fastjson2 groupId to com.alibaba.fastjson2. Placing this pom_patch before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade ensures the required classes are on the classpath when the migration recipes run and compile.

history.qwen_observation: The add_missing_deps step failed because Maven's local repository cached a negative resolution for com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail, blocking all subsequent version fetches.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the explicit dependency patch step bypasses the Maven cache blocker. The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe will manage Jakarta namespace migrations and dependency management via Spring Boot's BOM, allowing the build to proceed to the Java 21 upgrade without triggering the cached failure.

history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade recipe applied successfully but post-recipe compile failed because com.sun.mail.* (Jakarta Mail implementation) and com.alibaba.fastjson2.Feature classes are missing from the classpath
history.qwen_rationale: The SB 3.3 upgrade migrates javax.mail to jakarta.mail but the com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail implementation dependency fails to resolve from the internal Nexus (cached negative results for 2.1.2/2.1.3). Pre-patching the pom with jakarta.mail-api and com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail at version 2.1.1 (an uncached version) alongside forcing fastjson2 to 2.0.43 before the SB upgrade should satisfy the compile classpath requirements for the AOT hint sources and the vendored fastjson2 JSON.java that references Feature.

history.qwen_observation: Dependency resolution failed for com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail:2.1.1 due to internal Nexus cache blocking previously attempted versions (2.1.3, 2.1.2, 2.1.1).
history.qwen_rationale: Switching to version 2.1.0 bypasses the cached negative resolution entries while still providing a valid Jakarta Mail implementation compatible with Spring Boot 3.3, allowing the build to proceed before the framework migration.
